reasoning: In early 2026, Zhipu AI released GLM-5, a 744-billion-parameter frontier AI model reporting benchmark scores highly competitive with top global models (like GPT-5.1 or Claude Opus). According to multiple 2026 sources, the GLM-5 model was trained entirely on a cluster of 100,000 Huawei Ascend 910B chips. Crucially, contemporary 2026 sources state these chips were manufactured by SMIC using 7nm DUV lithography, expressly referring to SMIC's yield limits (30-50%). While reports from 2024 and 2025 revealed Huawei had stockpiled millions of TSMC 7nm dies for some of its Ascend production, the specific 100k cluster used for GLM-5 is widely credited in industry sources to SMIC-fabricated chips. Given GLM-5 is currently established as a frontier model (competing with global State-of-the-Art models) and its compute was ostensibly sourced >50% from domestic, Chinese-fabricated chips, it appears to fulfill the metaculus resolution criteria. The minor residual risk is if an investigation subsequently reveals these specific 100k chips were part of the older TSMC stockpile rather than SMIC fabrication, but current 2026 consensus strongly supports a domestic Chinese fabrication. Therefore, the probability of a positive resolution is high.
